name: Button
description: A styled button component for triggering actions. Built on Base UI Button with StyleX styling.

variant.description: Visual style of the button. Solid has a filled background, outline has a border with transparent background, ghost has no border or background.

color.description: Color palette applied to the button. Default uses the primary color, secondary uses the secondary palette, destructive uses the destructive/danger palette.

size.description: Size of the button affecting height, padding, and font size.

sx.description: StyleX styles for consumer overrides. Applied last for deterministic 'last style wins' behavior.

disabled.description: Whether the button is disabled. Reduces opacity and disables pointer events.

focusableWhenDisabled.description: Whether the button remains focusable when disabled.

dataAttributes.data-disabled: Present when the button is disabled.

intents.reasoning: Button is the primary component for triggering user actions. Use for any clickable action that is not navigation.
Confirmation actions use the default solid button to indicate the primary positive action.
Cancel actions use outline or ghost to de-emphasize relative to the primary action.
Destructive actions use the destructive color to warn users of irreversible consequences.
Secondary actions use outline or ghost variant to visually de-emphasize them relative to the primary action.

avoidWhen.scenario: Navigation to another page or URL
avoidWhen.reasoning: Use a link (<a> tag) or Link component instead. Buttons are for actions, links are for navigation. Screen readers distinguish between these.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use an anchor element or Link component with appropriate href.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use a Toggle or Switch component which communicates the on/off state to assistive technology.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use Toggle from @base-ui/react/toggle or a Switch component.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use RadioGroup, ToggleGroup, or Select instead. Buttons don't communicate selection state.
avoidWhen.alternative: Use RadioGroup, ToggleGroup, or Select components.

avoidWhen.reasoning: Use Menu.Trigger which handles aria-expanded, aria-haspopup, and keyboard navigation automatically.

examples.name: destructive-outline
examples.description: Destructive outline button for less prominent dangerous actions
